result:
Filled all 14 decisions at `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/agent-output/loom-charter-role-instructions.yaml`.

Highlights of the taste-level moves:

- **D1 (charter location)** → convention, no config (#18/#8 — don't build a config slot for a second consumer that doesn't exist).
- **D2 + D8 (caching strategy)** → read fresh per-weave (#19 — don't persist derived state; avoids the "restart to pick up edits" footgun).
- **D3 (field name)** → `instructionsFile` (#10 — no legacy in new stuff; #13 — ToolDefinition precedent is already load-bearing).
- **D5 (missing files)** → throw on configured, omit on default (#2 — fail loud where a user explicitly stated intent).
- **D10 (tool instructions in this commission)** → include (#36 — ship the coherent set; they're already resolved).
- **D13 (future-layer placeholders)** → just concatenate, no placeholders (#18 — no scaffolding for imagined consumers).
- **D14 (file-read testing)** → real temp dirs, no injection seam (#18/#8 — don't add abstraction for test-only reasons).

No `custom` answers were needed — every option set had a principled-aligned choice. Highest-confidence picks: D1, D3, D5, D11, D13. The markdown-vs-XML section-format call (D4) was the softest — picked markdown for coherence with the nested D12 shape.
